IPRoyal — Home Task — Gerardo Gomez

POV: You're a local artist
trying to go global 🎨

A mystery shopping analysis of the activation journey — told through the eyes of one very specific user.

scroll
01 — The persona

Meet Sara

Sara, 31
Ceramics artist · Tallinn, Estonia · Etsy + Shopify
Solo operator Instagram-first Semi-technical Handmade mugs
"I just want to see what my shop looks like to someone in New York."

Sara makes handmade mugs and sells them online. She's obsessive about the details — but everything she sees is through her own screen, her own location. She suspects that's not the full picture.

She doesn't need a tech tool. She needs a fair view of her own business.

Goal 01
See her real search ranking. Etsy shows different results by location. She wants the US view.
Goal 02
Check her storefront abroad. Does it look as intended to a buyer in New York?
Goal 03
Monitor competitors. Are they pricing differently by region? She has no way of knowing.
Goal 04
Stop deciding blind. Everything she sees is filtered through Tallinn. That's not the full picture.
02 — The journey

What Sara actually experienced

IPRoyal is built for technical users — developers, scrapers, data teams. Sara is not that. Here's what the journey looks like through her eyes.

1
She verifies her email and lands on the dashboard
A wizard opens — none of the options click for her
Six use-case tiles appear. Web Scraping, SEO Monitoring, Ad Verification. She doesn't see herself in any of them. She closes it and goes to Home.
2
She explores the dashboard
She notices her $10 balance and starts looking around
The home screen shows 5 proxy types with pricing. She doesn't know which one she needs. She picks Residential — it sounds the most familiar.
!
She's inside the Residential proxy page
"Do you need to keep the same IP?" — she guesses
No context, no plain-language explanation. Her answer directly determines which product she gets. She picks and hopes for the best.
Drop-off risk
!
Purchase screen
She has $10 and no idea if that's enough
Lowest preset is $30–$50/month. She doesn't know what she needs, what $10 gets her, or why she'd commit more before trying anything.
Major drop-off
!
She completes her first purchase
Paid. Now what?
Empty dashboard, upsell timer, no setup guide above the fold. The help email arrives 13 minutes later. Most users don't wait.
Biggest drop-off
03 — Communication flow audit

What Sara's inbox actually received.

Here's every email Sara received, in order — and what each one got right or wrong.

Fri 20 Mar · 11:34
Day 0
Complete your IPRoyal account setup
Verification email. Functional. 60-min link expiry is a subtle pressure point for someone who signs up and gets distracted.
Functional
Fri 20 Mar · 11:35
Day 0 +1 min
Your IPRoyal account is ready
Fires 1 min after verification. Right trigger, wrong message — "195+ countries" means nothing to Sara. No use-case framing.
Too generic
Fri 20 Mar · 12:07
Day 0 +33 min
Bonus money added to your IPRoyal account
$10 added. One CTA: "Access Dashboard." No context on what $10 buys, what to do next, or how to get started. Transactional without being useful.
Missing context
Sat 21 Mar · 11:35
Day 1
Rated 4.7/5 on G2 by 620+ proxy buyers
Social proof email sent 24h after signup — before Sara has had a single success moment. Proving credibility to someone who hasn't used the product yet is wasted signal.
Wrong timing
Sun 22 Mar · 11:35
Day 2
Test the full network for the price of one GB
Conversion push 48h after signup. Time-based, not behaviour-based. IPRoyal doesn't know Sara hasn't touched the product yet. Selling to someone who's already lost.
Behaviour-blind
Tue 24 Mar · 07:03
Post-purchase
Order confirmed — your 1 GB is ready
Good trigger, clean summary. But "next step" tells her to paste a proxy string into a tool. She doesn't have one.
Tone mismatch
Tue 24 Mar · 07:16
+13 min
Get connected in the next 5 minutes
Setup guide. Best email in the sequence — but still written for a developer. "Copy the formatted proxy string and paste into your tool or script." Sara doesn't have a tool or script.
Almost there
Critical gap
Every email after welcome fires on a time delay — regardless of what Sara did or didn't do. There is no behaviour-triggered communication. IPRoyal never knows she hasn't set up a proxy.
Critical gap
The setup email assumes developer knowledge. "Proxy string," "tool or script," "HTTP configuration" — none of this maps to Sara's mental model.
Opportunity
The "Get connected" email has the right intention. It just needs to be rewritten for a non-technical user — and triggered by purchase, not time.
04 — Communication flow

What would have helped Sara

A behaviour-triggered flow built around where Sara actually gets stuck — not a time-based drip sequence.

05 — Data & metrics

What we need to know about Sara

Behaviour-based triggers, not time delays. This is the data that makes it possible.

Events & attributes
Event
dashboard_visited — did she come back after signing up?
Event
proxy_purchased — what she bought, how much, and how she paid
Event
first_proxy_request — did she actually use it? This is the activation moment.
Attribute
use_case — captured in the wizard. What she's trying to do drives everything.
Attribute
technical_level — inferred from wizard answers. Determines how we talk to her.
How we measure success
30%
Activation rate
First proxy request within 7 days of signup
48h
Time to first use
Beyond 48h without use, intent drops sharply
40%
Setup email CTR
Post-purchase is peak intent — 40% is realistic
06 — Segmentation

Not everyone is Sara

The activation flow splits early based on two variables: technical level and use case. Sara sits in one specific quadrant.

Sara's segment
Non-technical · Marketplace seller
Needs to see results before she understands the tool. Highest drop-off risk.
Segment 2
Technical · Developer / Scraper
Knows exactly what he needs. Gets there fast, with little help.
Segment 3
Semi-technical · Social media manager
Understands the why, needs guidance on the how.
Segment 4
Enterprise · Large project
Activation happens through sales, not email.
07 — The fix

The email Sara should have received

Triggered immediately on purchase. Replaces the developer-focused "Get connected" email with something Sara can actually follow.

Written for someone who sells handmade mugs — not someone who writes Python scripts.

IPRoyal · hello@mail.iproyal.com Your 1GB is live. Here's how to use it.
IPRoyal
You're ready to go
Browse the web from anywhere in 3 steps.
Hi SaraPersonalised via {{first_name}} — captured at registration,

Your 1GBPersonalised via {{gb_amount}} — from purchase data of residential proxy traffic is ready. Here's the fastest way to use it — no technical setup required.
1
Install the IPRoyal browser extension. Works with Chrome and Firefox — takes about 30 seconds.
2
Select United StatesPre-filled via {{target_country}} — inferred from geo-restrictions use case selection from the extension and click Connect.
3
Open any website and see exactly what a local visitor sees — prices, listings, search results.
Install the browser extension →
Your 1GB{{gb_amount}} — from purchase data doesn't expire. Take your time. If anything doesn't work, reply to this email or use the live chat — we'll sort it in minutes.
IPRoyal · Triggered on purchase · Personalised for marketplace sellers
Why this moment
Purchase is peak motivation. She just spent money — she wants to see results immediately. This is the highest-attention moment in the entire journey.
Why this tone
No jargon. No proxy strings, no scripts. Personalised to first name + product type — data we actually have at purchase.
Deliverability
Sent from a dedicated address — not the main domain, so one bad send doesn't affect the brand. Standard authentication tells inbox providers it's legitimate. The content is mostly text with one CTA — nothing that looks like a sales blast. And because it's triggered by a purchase, it's expected. Relevant emails get opened, and opens build sender reputation over time.
08 — Challenges

What makes this hard

01
Behaviour data isn't being captured yet
The entire flow depends on events like first_proxy_request and dashboard_visited. If these aren't tracked, the decision nodes don't exist. Instrumentation comes first.
02
The Proxy Advisor doesn't capture use case in a usable way
Personalising emails by use case requires that data to be passed to the ESP. Right now the wizard answers likely don't flow into the CRM.
03
Small team, limited bandwidth
Building segmented flows for 4 user types requires content, engineering, and QA time. Priority: fix the post-purchase email first. Everything else is iteration.
04
Non-technical users are a new segment
IPRoyal's current communication is built for developers. Adapting tone and content for Sara without alienating existing users requires careful A/B testing.
